title: trifolium pratense var. rotundifolium
intro: Trifolium pratense var. rotundifolium, also known as the red clover, is a perennial legume native to Europe and Asia, characterized by its round leaflets and red flowers. The medicinal properties of red clover include isoflavones, which have been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of red clover include reducing symptoms of menopause, such as hot flashes and night sweats, as well as improving bone health and reducing the risk of heart disease. Red clover is commonly prepared as a herbal tea, capsule, or extract, and is often used as a natural remedy for various health conditions, including menopausal symptoms and osteoporosis.
